titles.title: scpviz: A Python bioinformatics toolkit for Single-cell Proteomics and multi-omics analysis

citation.article_title: SCANPY: Large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis
citation.author: Wolf
citation.unstructured_citation: Wolf, F. A., Angerer, P., & Theis, F. J. (2018). SCANPY: Large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis. Genome Biology, 19(1), 15. https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-017-1382-0

citation.article_title: Molecularly-guided spatial proteomics captures single-cell identity and heterogeneity of the nervous system
citation.author: Dutta
citation.unstructured_citation: Dutta, S., Pang, M., Coughlin, G. M., Gudavalli, S., Roukes, M. L., Chou, T.-F., & Gradinaru, V. (2025, February 11). Molecularly-guided spatial proteomics captures single-cell identity and heterogeneity of the nervous system. https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.02.10.637505

citation.article_title: Increasing Proteome Coverage Through a Reduction in Analyte Complexity in Single-Cell Equivalent Samples
citation.author: Pang
citation.unstructured_citation: Pang, M., Jones, J. J., Wang, T.-Y., Quan, B., Kubat, N. J., Qiu, Y., Roukes, M. L., & Chou, T.-F. (2025). Increasing Proteome Coverage Through a Reduction in Analyte Complexity in Single-Cell Equivalent Samples. Journal of Proteome Research, 24(4), 1528–1538. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jproteome.4c00062

citation.article_title: In-Depth Comparison of Reagent-Based Digestion Methods and Two Commercially Available Kits for Bottom-Up Proteomics
citation.author: Uslan
citation.unstructured_citation: Uslan, T., Quan, B., Wang, T.-Y., Pang, M., Qiu, Y., & Chou, T.-F. (2025). In-Depth Comparison of Reagent-Based Digestion Methods and Two Commercially Available Kits for Bottom-Up Proteomics. ACS Omega, 10(10), 10642–10652. https://doi.org/10.1021/acsomega.4c11585

citation.article_title: Accurate Label-Free Quantification by directLFQ to Compare Unlimited Numbers of Proteomes
citation.author: Ammar
citation.unstructured_citation: Ammar, C., Schessner, J. P., Willems, S., Michaelis, A. C., & Mann, M. (2023). Accurate Label-Free Quantification by directLFQ to Compare Unlimited Numbers of Proteomes. Molecular & Cellular Proteomics, 22(7). https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mcpro.2023.100581

citation.article_title: Fast, sensitive and accurate integration of single-cell data with Harmony
citation.author: Korsunsky

Fast, sensitive and accurate integration of single-cell data with Harmony. Nature Methods, 16(12), 1289–1296. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-019-0619-0

citation.article_title: DIA-NN: Neural networks and interference correction enable deep proteome coverage in high throughput
citation.author: Demichev

citation.article_title: STRING: A web-server to retrieve and display the repeatedly occurring neighbourhood of a gene

J., & von Mering, C. (2023). The STRING database in 2023: Protein-protein association networks and functional enrichment analyses for any sequenced genome of interest. Nucleic Acids Research, 51(D1), D638–D646. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkac1000

citation.article_title: The scverse project provides a computational ecosystem for single-cell omics data analysis

A., Finotello, F., Wolf, F. A., Yosef, N., Stegle, O., & Theis, F. J. (2023). The scverse project provides a computational ecosystem for single-cell omics data analysis. Nature Biotechnology, 41(5), 604–606. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41587-023-01733-8

citation.article_title: Parkinson’s disease modeling in regenerative spiny mice (Acomys dimidiatus) captures key disease-relevant behavioral, histological, and molecular signatures
